title: Bitter Kenyan Cacao Brew
description: A traditional Kenyan dish made with cacao and rendered fat, perfect for satisfying midnight cravings while adhering to Ayurvedic and sattvic dietary principles.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Use filtered water to maintain the brew's sattvic properties.

instructions: Combine cacao nibs, rendered beef tallow, water, ginger, cardamom pods, salt, and black pepper in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat, whisking constantly.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, but do not allow it to boil.
Continue to whisk the mixture for another 2-3 minutes, until it thickens slightly and the flavors meld together.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the Ayurvedic ghee, if using.
Allow the brew to cool slightly before serving.
Strain the brew through a fine-mesh sieve into a serving cup or mug.
Serve immediately, while still warm and molten.
Garnish with a sprinkle of cacao nibs, if desired.
Enjoy the rich, bitter flavors of the Bitter Kenyan Cacao Brew.
